---
title: "Tamara Aranguiz Rago, Ph.D."
subtitle: |
  Postdoctoral Associate  
  University of Colorado Boulder
toc: false
---

::: {.home-hero}
::: {.hero-copy}
## Tectonic geomorphology across timescales

I am a tectonic geomorphologist interested in how faults, climate, erosion, and sediment transport shape Earth's surface.

My research combines field observations, remote sensing, geochronology, thermochronology, and numerical modeling to understand how landscapes respond to tectonic and climatic forcing.

[Research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary} [Publications](publications.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-light}
:::
:::

::: {.caption}
Salar Grande Fault, Atacama Desert.
:::

::: {.intro-strip}
After completing my Ph.D. research in the hyperarid Atacama Desert, I am expanding my work toward glaciated and postglacial landscapes, sediment routing, and tectonic geomorphology in mountain systems.
:::

## Research themes

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.research-card}
![](assets/img/web/salar_grande_fault.jpg){.card-image fig-alt="Hyperarid landscape along the Salar Grande Fault in northern Chile"}

### Active faults and landscape response

Slowly slipping faults leave measurable records in topography, drainage networks, sediment deposits, and surface deformation.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.research-card}
![](assets/img/web/sat_image_salar_grande.jpg){.card-image fig-alt="Satellite view of the Salar Grande region"}

### Climate, sediment, and landscape evolution

Numerical models help test how climate variability and sediment cover influence river dynamics, divide migration, and fault-zone morphology.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.research-card}
![](assets/img/web/whidbey_landslides.jpg){.card-image fig-alt="Coastal bluffs and landslide deposits on Whidbey Island"}

### Long-term exhumation and mountain evolution

Thermochronology and geologic constraints reveal cooling, exhumation, basin development, and landscape evolution over millions of years.
:::
:::

:::

## Methods

::: {.method-list}
- Field geology and geomorphic mapping
- Satellite remote sensing, InSAR, GNSS, and topographic analysis
- Geochronology, thermochronology, and sediment records
- Landscape-evolution modeling and scientific programming
:::

::: {.feature-row}
::: {.feature-image}
![](assets/img/web/osl_salar_grande_2023.jpg){fig-alt="Tamara holding an OSL sample tube during fieldwork in the Atacama Desert"}
:::

::: {.feature-copy}
### Fieldwork, samples, and models

The work moves between landscapes, samples, satellite data, and code: collecting sediment records in the field, measuring surface deformation and topography, and testing process-based explanations with numerical models.
:::
:::
